data.body:
=== SESSION LOG — E1 finding-adjudication corpus: built -> INVALIDATED -> grounded rebuild prototyped -> REJECTED (budget). Outcome-corpus survives; epic 6982 needs a rewire. (2026-07-16) ===

## 1. WHAT E1 SET OUT TO DO
E1 (e95e, "freeze plan-review ground-truth corpora", child of epic 6982 = the R1-R7 plan-review calibration/decomposition epic) was to freeze TWO corpora:
  (a) outcome_corpus.jsonl  — one row per reviewed ticket (per-ticket outcome signals);
  (b) adjudication_corpus.jsonl — finding-level TP/FP GROUND-TRUTH labels, needed by 6 downstream siblings (E2 d125, E3 def5, E6 a880, R1 59b7, R2 b080, R7 b1a4 all depend_on E1).
Original AC gated the labels on an LLM-only Cohen's-kappa >= 0.7 ship/discard.

## 2. WHAT WAS BUILT (before the invalidation)
- outcome_corpus.jsonl: 628 rows, mined from the tickets-branch git objects. VALID + unaffected by everything below.
- adjudication_corpus.jsonl: 300 surfaced + 100 dropped findings (text-bearing, positionally bucketed by coverage.counts, seed-1729 stratified).
- Double-labeled by two LLM raters (A = claude-opus-4-8, B = claude-sonnet-5, blind); kappa.py stratified reporting.
- Operator-approved scope change: kappa>=0.7 gate -> honest kappa REPORTING + HUMAN adjudication of disputes. Reported kappa 0.472 / Gwet AC1 0.581 / raw agreement 0.766 (kappa paradox from TP-skew).
- 30 A/B disputes clustered (cluster_disputes.py); operator HAND-ADJUDICATED all 30 across 6 AskUserQuestion rounds (16 TP / 14 FP; 3 semantic groups + 4 precedent-inferred-and-confirmed); folded (apply_adjudications.py) -> 379 gold labels.
- README finalized, E1 ACs rewritten (kappa reporting + [operator-attested] human adjudication), committed as Gerrit change 735.

## 3. THE INVALIDATION (the pivot — operator-flagged)
The adjudication tooling did NOT match production, on TWO orthogonal axes:
  (1) TRUNCATION: adjudicate.py capped plan text at PLAN_CAP=6000 chars (adjudicate.py:58,97). Production (orchestrator.assemble_context, orchestrator.py:188-211) reviews the WHOLE plan + children, "ALWAYS whole; never truncated". 56% of the 400 findings sit on plans >6000 chars.
  (2) NO GROUNDING: adjudicate.py made a single _client.messages.create call with NO tools (adjudicate.py:120). Production runs 16 criteria (A1,E4,G1G2,G3,G4,G6,G7,T1,T3,T5c,T8,T10,T11,T13,T14,removal-rationale) as AGENTIC finders that grep the codebase (pai_tools.filesystem_tools+grounding_tools+rebar_tools, runner.py:240-242). 55% of findings are on such criteria; the rater could not do what production does.
  COMBINED: 316/400 (79%) findings contaminated; only 84 (21%) methodologically clean. Kappa double-labeled subset 82% contaminated.
  THE HUMAN LAYER IS ALSO INVALID: the 30 disputes were SELECTED by the faulty A/B process (both raters truncated+tool-less); the human was ANCHORED on faulty rater rationales; 17/30 disputes are on agentic criteria where neither rater nor the human systematically grounded in code.
  CONCRETE PROOF (S001, ticket e165): I ruled it "FP = escaped defect", claiming I had "verified" it. I verified the CODE fact (event_append.py:54 has its own EVENT_TYPES frozenset) but did NOT read the whole PLAN. e165's plan Section 5 "Write-path allowlist" (at char ~8166, PAST the 6000 cap) ALREADY names event_append.py + EVENT_TYPES + _validate_event and says BOTH sets must be updated. So the finding is a restatement absent only from the file_impact ARRAY, not an escaped defect. I made the EXACT "didn't read the whole plan" error the truncated rater made.
  CONSEQUENCE: every TP/FP label (LLM + human), disputes.jsonl, dispute_worksheet.md, the kappa figures, and the README validity section are INVALID. Gerrit change 735 was ABANDONED.
  SURVIVES: outcome_corpus.jsonl (event-mined, never LLM-labeled); the finding SAMPLE (production outputs; the 300+100 stratified draw is reusable); the harness/rubrics/SOTA report as machinery.

## 4. CORRECTED APPROACH — investigated, prototyped, validated, then REJECTED
Design: reuse production's own grounded Pass-2 verifier so the adjudicator is a faithful (not weaker) analog.
- Reuse seams found + used: orchestrator.assemble_context (whole context); a tiny reuse workflow (harnesses/verify_single_finding.yaml) running production's real ops plan_review_verify_inputs -> prompt plan-review-verifier-agentic (output_schema plan_review_verification) -> plan_review_decide; get_runner(cfg) in execution_mode=agentic binds pai_tools codebase access; gate_session() required to un-fail-close the agentic file tools.
- REVIEW-TIME-COMMIT grounding (critical): the sidecar records only the plan hash (material_fingerprint), NOT a code SHA. Recovered via the ticket's REVIEW_RESULT event timestamps -> the contemporaneous origin/main commit (git rev-list -1 --before). Validated: at e165's review-time SHA 6244666e5, KEY_ADD is ABSENT from event_append.py; at current HEAD it is PRESENT.
- Prototype (harnesses/grounded_adjudicate.py) + hardened batch runner (harnesses/grounded_batch_adjudicate.py) BUILT + validated. e165 smoke test: grounding at the review-time SHA (work not yet done) FLIPS the verdict dropped(validity 0.375)->advisory/surfaced(validity 0.5) vs the current-HEAD bias. Batching works (a118, 5 findings, one aggregate call). Cost ~$0.30/finding (variance $0.20-$1.60); full 400 ~ $70-130. Corpus scan: 195/195 tickets resolve a review SHA; 165/195 (85%) have plan_matches_review=True (faithfully relabelable); 30/195 (15%) have drifted plans and can't be faithfully relabeled without old-plan reconstruction.
- THE CIRCULARITY (why it was rejected): the grounded verifier IS production's own Pass-2, so its output = the gate's own answer, ALREADY persisted in the historical REVIEW_RESULT sidecars (validity/impact/decision per finding, from the current gate since it was last updated). Re-running it is DUPLICATIVE and cannot independently validate the gate. The only independent ground truth is HUMAN adjudication (grounded), which exceeds the available human time/attention budget.
  OPERATOR DECISION (2026-07-16): REJECT the grounded rebuild for now. LLM spend duplicates existing sidecar evidence; human review over budget. Some work may move out of scope.

## 5. STATE OF FILES (all uncommitted on branch corpus/e1; Gerrit 735 ABANDONED)
- VALID + landable: docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md; runs/outcome_corpus.jsonl; harnesses/mine_outcome_corpus.py.
- INVALID (do not ship): runs/adjudication_corpus.jsonl, runs/disputes.jsonl, runs/dispute_worksheet.md, runs/README.md validity section; the TP/FP labels produced by harnesses/{adjudicate,cluster_disputes,apply_adjudications,kappa}.py.
- Prototype-only (not production): harnesses/{grounded_adjudicate,grounded_batch_adjudicate}.py, harnesses/verify_single_finding.yaml; grounded labels in $CLAUDE_JOB_DIR/tmp (ephemeral).

## 6. WHAT REMAINS TO CLOSE THE CALIBRATION EPIC (6982)
- Rescope E1: land the VALID outcome_corpus + mining harness + SOTA report; DROP/DEFER the finding-adjudication corpus + kappa deliverables from E1's ACs.
- Re-examine the 6 E1 dependents (E2,E3,E6,R1,R2,R7): most likely need the OUTCOME corpus or the SIDECAR evidence, NOT the invalid finding-adjudication TP/FP labels. In particular E2 (seeded-defect benchmark) yields finding-level ground truth BY CONSTRUCTION (known seeded defects) and E6 (judge self-consistency) measures reliability WITHOUT ground truth -- both are MORE valid ground-truth mechanisms than the LLM-adjudicated corpus. Rewire dependents onto these + the sidecar evidence; defer any that hard-require an independent finding-level TP/FP gold set.
- Capture the methodological lesson (candidate ADR): an LLM adjudication/ground-truth process must be at least as capable (grounded, whole-context) as the process it judges, or its labels are invalid; and re-running the gate's own verifier is circular. Prefer CONSTRUCT-VALID ground truth (seeded defects) + outcome signals + the gate's persisted sidecar self-assessment over LLM-adjudicated finding labels.
- Audit sibling experiments across the epic for the SAME tool-less/truncated confound before they build on any LLM-labeled corpus.
